action.reasoning: Observation: Latest full custom per-target pose matching (rel_az=(target_az-input_az)%360, validated renderer call, white-BG input, robust camera func) completes val successfully but LPIPS=0.6124 (worse than best 0.6021 from iter16 with partial fallback orbit). Custom rays match inspected TripoSR source but dataset camera conventions may need azimuth offset (common in such models: input assumed at az=0/180). Analysis: Slight misalignment persists (dataset view0 az varies widely; elev near +/-90 can still be unstable despite eps/mask). Zero-shot TripoSR+exact per-view rays remains optimal approach (<1B params, no training). Val has GT target_views so we can locally optimize azimuth_offset (-180 to 180 step 30) + white-bg composite for rendered outputs by computing LPIPS/PSNR/SSIM vs GT using lpips lib on 20 val objs (fast). This finds best config before submit_eval. Decision: Write val_eval.py (full reusable get func + renderer call from inspect, offset sweep, metrics vs GT targets, report best), run it to identify optimal offset (expect LPIPS drop), then update predict.py with best offset + white-bg composite for renders + full robustness, test on val, submit_eval for improved metric+time.
action.command: python val_eval.py && echo 'Best offset saved' && python predict.py --data_path /data/val --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output val_predictions.json --num_objects 5
